title: Calculating your CGT
description: How to calculate your capital gains tax (CGT), including how to use capital losses and work out your cost base.
keywords: rollover extension, apply for extension to CGT rollover, cgt rollover, cgt rollover relief

child_links.title: How to calculate your CGT
child_links.description: Use the calculator or steps to work out your CGT, including your capital proceeds and cost base.

child_links.title: Capital proceeds from disposing of assets
child_links.description: Money and other types of capital proceeds, market valuation of an asset, and reductions to your capital proceeds.

child_links.title: Cost base of assets
child_links.description: Work out the cost base of an asset, including foreign currency and excluded amounts, and when not to use the cost base.

child_links.title: Cost base adjustments for capital works
child_links.description: Capital works deductions can't be included in the cost base or reduced cost base of an asset.

child_links.title: Indexing the cost base
child_links.description: If you acquired an asset before 21 September 1999, you can index its cost base for inflation to reduce capital gains.

child_links.title: Using capital losses to reduce capital gains
child_links.description: Find out when you can use capital losses to reduce your capital gains, and how to carry forward a net capital loss.
